title: rubus yabei f. eglandulosus
intro: Rubus yabei f. eglandulosus, also known as Japanese black raspberry, is a rare and unique variety of black raspberry native to Japan, characterized by its lack of glandular hairs. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus yabei f. eglandulosus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or jam, and is also used in traditional Japanese medicine, known as Kampo.
